name: Microsoft To Do MCP Server
what_it_does: Microsoft To Do MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Microsoft To Do task management — creating and updating tasks, reading task lists, setting due dates and reminders, and integrating Microsoft's task management platform (powered by Microsoft Graph API) into agent-driven productivity and workflow automation.
use_cases: Creating tasks in Microsoft To Do from productivity agents
Reading and updating daily task lists from planning agents
Setting due dates and priorities from scheduling agents
Integrating Outlook task capture with Microsoft 365 from workflow agents
Building task capture pipelines from email or calendar agents
Syncing tasks across Microsoft ecosystem from integration agents
not_for: Teams using Todoist, Things 3, or other task managers — use appropriate MCPs
Complex project management (use Jira or Linear for structured projects)
best_when: A Microsoft 365 user wants AI agents to create, read, and manage tasks in Microsoft To Do as part of their productivity workflow.
avoid_when: You don't use Microsoft 365, or need team task management with more structure — use Jira, Linear, or Todoist MCPs.

auth.notes: Microsoft Graph API OAuth 2.0 required. Azure AD app registration needed. Tasks.ReadWrite permission scope. MSAL authentication flow.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Microsoft To Do: free with Microsoft account
pricing.notes: Microsoft To Do is free. Microsoft Graph API free for basic usage. Azure AD app registration free. Community MCP is free.

performance.rate_limits: Microsoft Graph API: 10,000 requests per 10 minutes per app

known_agent_gotchas: Azure AD app registration required — non-trivial setup with OAuth redirect URIs
Token refresh must be handled — access tokens expire in 1 hour
Microsoft Graph throttling uses exponential backoff — handle 429 responses
Task list IDs needed for most operations — discover lists first before creating tasks
Community MCP — Microsoft Graph API versioning may require updates
Personal vs work Microsoft accounts have different Graph API behaviors
agent_readiness.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 via Microsoft identity platform. HTTPS enforced. Store client secrets in secure vault. Minimum required Graph API scopes. Community MCP. Personal task data — handle with appropriate privacy.

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 Microsoft identity. HTTPS enforced. Secure client secret storage. Minimum scope. Community MCP.
